DEFENDANT IS NOT ENTITLED TO RECOVER THE FOLLOWING COSTS: MEDIATION EXPENSES, COURIER COSTS AND VIDEOTAPE SYNCHRONIZATION SERVICE. DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER THE FOLLOWING COSTS: DEPOSITIONS, $18,208.23; SERVICE OF PROCESS, $595.00; WITNESS FEES, $835.55, TOTALING $19,638.78. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS COUNTY OF ALAMEDA AND ALAMEDA COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS

No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion to tax costs is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is not entitled to recover the following costs: mediation expenses, courier costs and videotape synchronization service. Defendant is entitled to recover the following costs: Depositions, $18,208.23; service of process, $595.00; witness fees, $835.55, totaling $19,638.78. Judgment in favor of defendants for costs is stayed pending the outcome of plaintiff's appeal. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Nov-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORINA, COUNTY OF ALAMEDA'S (1)DEMURRER, AND (2) MOTION TO STRIKE REGARDING SECOND AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Ingrid M. Evans, Esq. & Jonathan V. Holtzman, Esq. of Renne Sloan Holtzman Sakai LLP 678-3800 for pltfs & opposing parties; Arturo J. Gonzalez, Esq. of Morrison & Foerster LLP 268-7020 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUsTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN 10 DAYS AS TO THE BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT CLAIM TO PLEAD CONDUCT THAT DOES NOT SIMPLY RESTATE THE CONTRACT CLAIM. OVERRULED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS. E.H. MORRILLO V. SATE OF CALIFORNIA (1976) 65 C.2ND 787,794 AUTHORIZES A SUIT BASED ON CONTRACT CLAIMS WHICH IS THE BASIS OF THE ESTOPPEL CLAIM. THE DISCLOSURES IN THE GOVERNMENT CLAIM ARE ADEQUATE UNDER STOCKETT V. ASS'N OF CAL. WATER AGENCIES. (2004) 34 C. 4TH 441,446. DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT BASED ON THE FAILURE TO FOLLOW THE PRESCRIBED CONTRACTING METHOD WAS NOT RAISED IN THE MOVING PAPERS AND IS NOT CONSIDERED BY COURT. ATTORNEY'S FEES MAY BE RECOVERED UNDER CCP 1021.5. In addition, the Court granted the motion to strike re CCP 1033. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
